name: Knees Cave In (Valgus Collapse)
description: During squat or lunge patterns, knees collapse medially past the toes. Indicates glute medius/maximus weakness and adductor/TFL dominance.

inhibit.instructions: Foam roll each area for 30-60s, pausing on tender spots for 30s.

lengthen.instructions: Static stretch each muscle 30s, 2 sets. Breathe into the stretch.

activate.instructions: 2 sets of 12-15 reps, slow tempo (4/2/1). Focus on mind-muscle connection.

integrate.instructions: Perform squat with mini-band above knees to cue abduction. 2 sets of 12.

assessmentCue: Watch knees during overhead squat or single-leg squat. If medial knee displacement >2 inches, this compensation is active.
source: NASM CPT 7th Edition — Overhead Squat Assessment

name: Feet Turn Out (Duck Feet)
description: During squat or walking, feet externally rotate past 10 degrees. Indicates lateral calf/biceps femoris tightness and medial stabilizer weakness.

inhibit.instructions: Foam roll lateral calves and lateral hamstrings 30-60s each. Focus on outer fibers.

lengthen.instructions: Static stretch calves (lateral bias) and lateral hamstrings 30s, 2 sets.

activate.instructions: Single-leg calf raises with toes slightly inward. Tibialis raises for anterior balance. 2x15.

integrate.instructions: Squat with feet straight (tape lines on floor as guide). 2 sets of 12.

assessmentCue: Place tape on floor parallel. Have client squat with feet on tape. If feet rotate out past 10°, compensation is active.
source: NASM CPT 7th Edition — Overhead Squat Assessment

name: Excessive Forward Lean
description: During squat, torso leans forward excessively (more than ~30° from vertical). Indicates calf/hip flexor tightness and glute/erector weakness.

inhibit.instructions: Foam roll calves and hip flexors 30-60s each. Use ball on TFL.

lengthen.instructions: Static stretch calves and hip flexors 30s, 2 sets each.

activate.instructions: Glute bridges 2x15 with 2s squeeze. Prone cobra 2x10 with 3s hold.

integrate.instructions: Goblet squat with anterior load forces upright posture. 2 sets of 12, slow tempo.

assessmentCue: Side view during squat. If shoulders fall significantly forward of hips, compensation is active.
source: NASM CPT 7th Edition — Overhead Squat Assessment

name: Low Back Arches (Anterior Pelvic Tilt / Excessive Lordosis)
description: During squat or overhead movements, low back hyperextends. Classic lower crossed syndrome. Indicates hip flexor/erector dominance and glute/core weakness.

inhibit.instructions: Foam roll hip flexors (TFL area), lats, and thoracic spine 30-60s each.

lengthen.instructions: Kneeling hip flexor stretch 30s each side. Lat stretch 30s each side.

activate.instructions: Dead bug 2x8 each side. Glute bridge 2x15. Pelvic tilts 2x10.

integrate.instructions: Ball squat against wall with focus on maintaining neutral pelvis. 2 sets of 12.

assessmentCue: Side view during squat. If visible lordotic curve increases during descent, compensation is active. Also check standing posture — hand should not fit between low back and wall beyond 1 inch.
source: NASM CPT 7th Edition — Overhead Squat Assessment, Janda Lower Crossed Syndrome

name: Arms Fall Forward
description: During overhead squat assessment, arms drift forward past the ears. Indicates lat/pec tightness and mid-back weakness.

inhibit.instructions: Foam roll lats 30-60s each side. Ball on pecs against wall 30-60s each side.

lengthen.instructions: Lat stretch (kneeling, arms on bench) 30s each side. Doorway pec stretch 30s at 3 angles.

activate.instructions: Prone cobra 2x10. Prone Y raises 2x10. Band pull-aparts 2x15.

integrate.instructions: Row with full scapular retraction. 2 sets of 12. 2:1 pull-to-push ratio in all sessions.

assessmentCue: During overhead squat, arms should stay in line with ears (side view). If arms drift forward of ear line, compensation is active.
source: NASM CPT 7th Edition — Overhead Squat Assessment

name: Shoulders Elevate (Shrug During Movement)
description: During squat, pressing, or carrying, shoulders hike toward ears. Indicates upper trap/levator dominance and lower trap/serratus weakness.

inhibit.instructions: Foam roll upper traps with ball against wall 30-60s each side. Gentle — this area is often sensitive.

lengthen.instructions: Upper trap stretch (ear to shoulder) 30s each side. Levator stretch (nose to armpit) 30s each side.

activate.instructions: Prone cobra 2x10 — depress shoulders deliberately. Serratus wall slides 2x10 — reach at top.

integrate.instructions: Wall angels 2x10 — keep shoulders DOWN while arms move. If shoulders hike, reduce ROM.

assessmentCue: Watch shoulders during overhead squat or pressing. If shoulders visibly rise toward ears during the movement, compensation is active.
commonCauses: Overactive upper traps from desk work
source: NASM CPT 7th Edition — Overhead Squat Assessment

name: Head Juts Forward (Forward Head Posture)
description: Head translates anteriorly past the shoulder line. Indicates deep cervical flexor weakness and upper trap/SCM/suboccipital tightness.
underactive: Deep Cervical Flexors (Longus Colli, Longus Capitis)

inhibit.instructions: Place lacrosse ball at base of skull (suboccipitals). Gentle pressure 30-60s. Also roll upper traps with ball against wall.

lengthen.instructions: Upper trap stretch 30s each. Levator stretch 30s each. SCM stretch (look up and to the side) 30s each.

activate.instructions: Chin tucks 3x10 with 5s hold — train deep cervical flexors. Prone cobra 2x10 — depress and retract.

integrate.instructions: Wall angels with head against wall (chin tucked). 2x10. Head must maintain wall contact throughout.

assessmentCue: Side view standing: ear should be over shoulder. If ear is visibly anterior to shoulder, forward head posture is present.
source: NASM CPT 7th Edition — Static Posture Assessment, Janda Upper Crossed Syndrome
